items.title: Lima 2027 promises biggest defence showcase, 400,000 visitors expected
items.summary: Malek, while delivering the speech of Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Khaled Nordin at the launch event today, said Lima 2027 aims to strengthen Malaysia's position as a strategic defence, maritime and aerospace hub in the Asia-Pacific region. This, he said, aligned with the National Defence Policy, the National Defence Industry Policy and the National Defence Strategic Plan. It said the exhibition would also strengthen defence diplomacy, expand defence industry supply chains and create new opportunities for Malaysian companies to penetrate international markets.

items.title: In Larkin, land leases and a city’s growing pains shape Johor election contest
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Land lease renewals in Kampung Melayu Majidee and the need to modernise public infrastructure have emerged as the key issues shaping the contest for the Larkin state seat in the 16th Johor state election. Mohd Hairi, who is also State Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman, said the state government remained committed to safeguarding Kampung Melayu Majidee through lease renewal offers of between 60 and 99 years, either on an individual or lot basis, as well as a 50 per cent premium discount to ease the financial burden on residents. Suhaizan, who is Pulai MP, however, said the state government’s measures had yet to meet residents’ expectations.

items.title: Alliance Bank expands financial literacy drive for students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd has expanded its financial literacy programme for students, including secondary schools and introduced biodiversity education. The bank's eighth edition of the AEIOU Seeds for Life programme will also be extended to schools in the east coast as well as selected international and private schools for the first time. The programme includes two national competitions: the AEIOU Eco-Comic Challenge for primary school students and the AEIOU Eco-Pitching Challenge for secondary school students, offering a total prize pool of RM35,000.

items.title: MACC: Three arrested in Kedah over alleged RM20m financing bid for paddy and rice purchases that never happened
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Three people, including two company directors, have been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in Kedah over allegations they used false documents in an attempt to obtain about RM20 million in financing for paddy and rice purchases that investigators say never took place. In a statement today, the anti-graft agency said the suspects, who are involved in the rice and paddy production and processing business, are suspected of submitting documents containing false particulars to a development financial institution under the Trade Working Capital Financing-I facility. The financing, worth about RM20 million, was intended for the purchase of paddy and rice.

items.title: DOE denies publishing viral cleanliness ranking infographic
items.summary: "The public is advised not to disseminate the information or use it as an official reference without first verifying it with the department. The department said the spread of unverified information could not only mislead the public but also potentially erode public confidence in official information relating to environmental management and conservation. To avoid confusion, the DOE said all official media statements, reports, statistics, infographics, announcements and information issued by the department would only be published through its official communication channels, namely its official portal at www.doe.gov.my, Facebook (@JabatanAlamSekitar), Instagram (@jabatanalamsekitar), X (@jasmalaysia), TikTok (@jas_malaysia_official) and YouTube (@JabatanAlamSekitar).

items.title: Malaysia's navy faces 2-year wait for new missile system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Replacing the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system could take at least two years to complete, with the Armed Forces working to address a capability gap affecting the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), says Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman. He said the delay was a setback as commissioned navy ships currently lacked missile capability. Malek said the timeline would depend on the procurement process and available budget.

items.title: ‘The tears just won’t stop’: Father says Banting stabbing victim leaves ICU but remains traumatised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The 15-year-old girl injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting remains deeply traumatised despite her condition stabilising after surgery, her father said today. The tears just won’t stop flowing,” he said. Mohd Firuz said doctors and nurses were continuing to monitor the teenager’s condition.

items.title: E-waste container misdeclared as tin ore catches fire at Port Klang
items.summary: PORT KLANG: A shipping container falsely declared as carrying tin ore caught fire shortly after arriving at Port Klang, where inspections later revealed it was filled with electronic waste (e-waste). Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the container arrived from overseas on July 6 with a declared cargo weight of 17,237kg. "The inspection found the container was filled entirely with electrical and electronic waste, not tin ore as declared in the shipping documents," he said.

items.title: Zambry: New higher education programmes in Sabah will target state's economic needs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is open to applications for new academic programmes in Sabah to broaden study options at public higher education institutions (IPTA) in the state. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said applications for programmes at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), polytechnics, and community colleges would be assessed based on institutional expertise, industry needs, student demand, and graduate employability. Zambry said the ministry is committed to expanding higher education opportunities in the state.

items.title: Perak Ruler officiates Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta
items.summary: IPOH, July 7 — The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, today officially opened the Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta here. The Raja Permaisuri of Perak, Tuanku Zara Salim, the Raja Muda of Perak, Raja Jaafar Raja Muda Musa, and the Raja DiHilir of Perak, Raja Iskandar Dzurkarnain Sultan Idris Shah also attended the event. In his speech, Saifuddin Nasution said the opening of the camp symbolises the MADANI Government’s commitment to strengthening the capabilities of the nation’s security forces to address increasingly complex and evolving security challenges.

items.title: Malaysia ranked world's 6th-best medical tourism destination, led by Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Malaysia has been ranked the world's sixth-best medical tourism destination, with Penang continuing to lead the country's healthcare travel sector. "Congratulations, Malaysia, and congratulations, Penang! Wong said Penang remained Malaysia's leading medical tourism destination, contributing about 45 per cent of the country's total medical tourism revenue.

items.title: Onn Hafiz: BN won't be complacent despite poll forecast
items.summary: PONTIAN: Barisan Nasional (BN) will not be complacent despite a survey projecting a comfortable victory in Saturday's state election, says Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi. With four days of campaigning remaining before polling on July 11, Onn Hafiz said BN's priority was to reach as many voters as possible across the state. He also urged voters to back BN's Pekan Nanas candidate Tan Eng Meng, describing him as a hardworking leader who had consistently served the constituency since the last election.

items.title: Election Commission says 588 complaints lodged as Johor polls enter final stretch
items.summary: KLUANG, July 7 — The Election Commission (EC) has received 588 complaints involving various election offences during the campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, as of today. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said that, of the total, 44 police reports had been lodged. Earlier, Ramlan, accompanied by Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman, monitored the smooth conduct of the polling process at several early voting centres, including the Kluang District Police Headquarters and Kem Mahkota.

items.title: Bank Negara international reserves rise to US$132.6bil at end-June
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia's international reserves rose to US$132.6 billion as at June 30, 2026, slightly more than the US$130.5 billion recorded as at June 15. In a statement today, the central bank said the reserves level had taken into account the quarterly foreign exchange revaluation changes. Bank Negara said the reserves position was sufficient to finance 4.7 months of imports of goods and services and was equivalent to 0.9 times the country's total short-term external debt.

items.title: Government studies Child Act reforms over indefinite detention of children – Kulasegaran
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Forty people remain detained under the Child Act 2001, with some held for up to 25 years, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the detainees comprised four individuals held for less than a year, 14 for between one and five years, seven for five to 10 years, eight for 10 to 15 years, four for 15 to 20 years, and three for 20 to 25 years. He said the government is studying amendments to the Act to align it with the best interests of children and international standards.

items.title: MASkargo, Qatar Airways Cargo launch KL-Bengaluru-Doha freighter link
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MAB Kargo Sdn Bhd (MASkargo) has expanded its partnership with Qatar Airways Cargo and launched a twice-weekly freighter service linking Kuala Lumpur, Bengaluru and Doha. The service will be operated using Qatar Airways' Boeing 777 freighters, adding 200 tonnes of weekly cargo capacity across the network for shipments including general cargo, perishables, pharmaceuticals and valuables. MASkargo, the logistics arm of Malaysia Aviation Group, said the partnership combines its extensive Asian network with Qatar Airways Cargo's global reach.

items.title: RNG Tech targets 1,570 new massage chairs to drive regional expansion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RNG Tech Bhd, the operator of the Rest N Go vending massage chair network, plans to deploy about 1,570 vending massage chairs across Malaysia and overseas markets as it enters its next phase of regional growth. The rollout will comprise 360 chairs in Malaysia and 450 units across overseas markets, including Singapore (100 units), Thailand (200 units), Cambodia (100 units) and Brunei (50 units). As of May 18, RNG Tech owned and operated 5,611 vending massage chairs across Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Cambodia and Brunei.

items.title: Johor election: 588 election offences reported as of today, says EC
items.summary: Ramlan also said the commission was maintaining its projection of a 96 per cent turnout for early voting today. Ramlan said early voter turnout stood at 82 per cent as of 1pm today. "Sixty-four early voting centres were opened across Johor about 8am today.

items.title: Environment Department denies issuing viral 2024 state cleanliness infographic
items.summary: “The public is advised not to disseminate the information or use it as an official reference without first verifying it with the department. The department said the spread of unverified information could not only mislead the public but also potentially erode public confidence in official information relating to environmental management and conservation. To avoid confusion, the DOE said all official media statements, reports, statistics, infographics, announcements and information issued by the department would only be published through its official communication channels, namely its official portal at www.doe.gov.my, Facebook (@JabatanAlamSekitar), Instagram (@jabatanalamsekitar), X (@jasmalaysia), TikTok (@jas_malaysia_official) and YouTube (@JabatanAlamSekitar).

items.title: Johor election: BN Youth raises concerns over government agencies' role in election dialogue
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) Youth has questioned the involvement of government agencies and civil servants in a state election dialogue scheduled at the RTM Johor Auditorium tonight. Its chief, Noor Azleen Ambros, raised concerns over the neutrality of public institutions during the campaign period after a notice issued by the Johor Broadcasting Department listed about 200 participants. This includes civil servants and representatives from agencies such as the Johor National Security Council, Community Communications Department (J-KOM) and the Prime Minister's Department Implementation Coordination Unit in Johor.

items.title: International students offer much in a borderless world
items.summary: Nevertheless, there remains a perception that the presence of international students reduces opportunities for Malaysian students to gain admission to higher education institutions. Consequently, the presence of international students does not diminish local students' access to higher education. More importantly, international students enrich the overall learning experience on campus.

items.title: VAT 69: Five decades of defending Malaysia from insurgents, terrorists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 69 Commando, popularly known as Very Able Troopers 69 (VAT 69), is Malaysia's premier special operations unit, operating under the police Special Operations Command (PGK). The unit was modelled closely after the British 22nd Special Air Service (22 SAS) Regiment and the New Zealand SAS, whose advisers played a significant role in shaping its early training and doctrine. Earlier today, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, officially opened the Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp , marking a major milestone in strengthening the country's elite counter-terrorism force.

items.title: South Korean stocks tumble as AI earnings boom faces doubts
items.summary: The MSCI EM Asia index fell nearly three per cent, marking its weakest session in nearly two weeks, as chip-heavy KOSPI index tumbled eight per cent to approach a one-month low. Taiwan's benchmark gauge also slipped more than two per cent. Singaporean stocks rose about one per cent to a record high, as shares of OCBC, UOB and DBS gained one per cent to two per cent.

items.title: HSBC reins in riskier private credit lending
items.summary: It will instead focus on lower-risk private credit funds, the report added. Exposure to private credit lending has troubled Europe's biggest lender and added to the market jitters around the sector. The FT report underscores the growing scrutiny of private credit portfolios, with regulators worldwide becoming increasingly concerned about banks' exposure to the US$3.5 trillion private credit industry.

items.title: Johor candidates pause campaigning to observe early voting by security personnel
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Despite their busy campaign schedule ahead of polling day this Saturday, Johor state election candidates took time to observe the early voting process for security personnel today. All three candidates for the Tenggaroh seat — Muhamad Amerul Muhamad (PN), Mohd Youzaimi Yusof (BN) and Md Yusof Dawam (PH) — also did likewise, at the Iskandar Camp early voting centre in Mersing. Besides candidates, top military and security officers also visited early voting centres, including Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman together with 21st Special Service Group (21 GGK) commander Major General Datuk Ahmad Shuhaimi Mat Wajab at Iskandar Camp, Mersing.

items.title: Former BH journalist Jailani Harun dies at 66
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Berita Harian journalist Datuk Jailani Harun, who served as a diplomatic officer to Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, died at the Serdang Hospital today. The news of Jailani's death at 11.34am was shared by the Deputy Prime Minister's office and confirmed by his eldest daughter, Nabilla, 33. Meanwhile, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil conveyed his condolences to Jailani's family and friends.

items.title: Lynas, South Korea firm sign deal for magnet factory in Malaysia [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Australian rare earths company Lynas said Tuesday it has partnered with South Korea's JS Link to build a permanent magnet factory in eastern Malaysia, a move that links its raw material production with downstream manufacturing. Lynas, which operates the largest commercial rare earths processing plant outside China, said it will invest about US$35 million in the facility near its site in Kuantan, around 250 kilometres (160 miles) east of Kuala Lumpur. Malaysia has around 274,144 metric tonnes of rare earth minerals, Malaysia's Environmental Sustainability Minister said in January.

items.title: Johor election: PKR's Shamsul under fire over talkin-inspired video
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: An election campaign video by PKR leader Datuk Seri Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, featuring elements resembling the Islamic talkin recitation, has sparked criticism online. The use of the talkin concept has drawn criticism on social media, with several users questioning whether a religious practice should be used as a political campaign device. The backlash intensified after he appeared to encourage voters to accept cash and gifts from rival parties, a practice condemned in Islam.

items.title: Govt reviewing indefinite detention of juvenile offenders under Child Act, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government is reviewing Section 97 of the Child Act 2001 (Act 611), including introducing a clearer detention period, to strike a balance between justice for victims, public safety and the rehabilitation of child offenders. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the review is being undertaken by a special committee formed following the abolition of the mandatory death penalty and life imprisonment in 2023. Kulasegaran said one of the key reasons for reviewing the provision was the situation faced by individuals who were detained as children without a clearly defined detention period.

items.title: 71,101 holiday homes generated RM2.2bil revenue in 2025: DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 71,101 holiday homes were operating nationwide in 2025, generating an estimated RM2.2 billion in revenue, according to the Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 published by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today. "The holiday homes industry continues to create positive economic spillover effects through higher demand for accommodation, transportation, food and beverage services, retail trade and recreational activities. The Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 is the first official statistical publication on holiday homes by state and district in Malaysia.

items.title: Full results of Johor election to be known by 10pm, says EC
items.summary: KLUANG: The full result of the Johor election, which goes to the polls this Saturday, is expected to be known by 10pm. Election Commission chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the final outcome could be determined by 10pm following an upgrade to the commission's existing system. "Let us pray together that with this upgraded system, we can achieve the full results by 10pm.

items.title: Father of stabbed Banting schoolgirl says he is willing to meet suspect’s family
items.summary: BANTING, July 7 — The father of the 15-year-old girl who was injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting has said he is willing to meet the family of the teenage suspect, adding that he hopes his daughter receives “the justice she deserves”. According to Sinar Harian , Mohd Firuz Ibrahim, 43, said he had not received any contact from the suspect’s family since yesterday’s attack. Mohd Firuz said he hoped appropriate action would be taken against those responsible and that the incident would serve as a lesson, given that it occurred on school grounds.

items.title: Armed forces reviewing training SOPs after Hobart Camp incidents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The armed forces are reviewing its training standard operating procedures (SOPs) to strengthen safety measures and facilities while ensuring operational readiness following two explosion incidents at Hobart Camp, Gurun. Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman said the review would focus on identifying areas for improvement in training procedures without compromising the military's combat readiness. He also directed the armed forces to reassess its training procedures following two separate explosion incidents at the camp.

items.title: ‘My daughter is not a bully,’ says father of girl stabbed at Banting school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The father of the 15-year-old girl stabbed at a secondary school in Banting has denied social media allegations that his daughter was involved in bullying, describing her as a reserved teenager and urging the public to leave the investigation to police. According to Sinar Harian , Mohd Firuz Ibrahim, 43, said he had seen WhatsApp messages and a viral list of names linking his daughter to alleged bullying, but stressed that neither he nor his family could verify their authenticity. Describing his daughter’s personality, Mohd Firuz said she rarely went anywhere without one of her parents.

items.title: Service as a lifetime commitment
items.summary: For several years, many of us watched Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye serve with tireless dedication during his tenure as a Member of Parliament. Late last evening, i made a phone call to Lee and raised a pressing matter, hoping for his action sometime later. Lee's lifelong dedication and Chew's rapid, heartfelt response show us what is possible when empathy is combined with action.

items.title: IHH Healthcare backs Merdeka 118 tower race to promote preventive health
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IHH Healthcare Malaysia is strengthening its preventive healthcare efforts through a three-year partnership as title partner of The IHH Sky Race | Merdeka 118. IHH Healthcare Malaysia chief executive officer Dr Kamal Amzan said the company's role in healthcare extends beyond treating patients, focusing also on prevention and healthier communities. Beyond The IHH Sky Race | Merdeka 118, Kamal said IHH was looking at supporting more initiatives that encourage healthier communities, including collaborations with other major sporting events.

items.title: DOSM: Malaysia’s health inflation doubles to 3pc in 2025 on higher insurance and service costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — Health inflation in Malaysia increased to three per cent in 2025 from 1.4 per cent the previous year, driven by rising healthcare service costs. Meanwhile, inflation for medicines rose to 2.7 per cent from 2.2 per cent in 2024, while health equipment increased to 1.2 per cent from 0.6 per cent. He said health inflation rates in selected countries in 2025 ranged between negative 0.8 per cent and 5.3 per cent.

items.title: Anwar backs safeguards against arbitrary action on journalists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Action against any journalist or registered media organisation should not be taken arbitrarily, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Instead, he said, any such action should first be referred to the Malaysian Media Council (MMM) before further measures are taken. Anwar said the mechanism was established to ensure that any action against media practitioners is carried out in a fair, transparent and independent manner.

items.title: Economy minister: Govt prioritises reducing aid exclusion errors as Malaysia prepares for ageing society
items.summary: He said preparations for an ageing nation required continuous economic and fiscal policies, as well as coordination of five key pillars under the National Ageing Action Plan (NAB) to safeguard the people’s well-being. “...we are developing policies to manage the challenges of an ageing population because Malaysia has already become an ageing nation. Replying to a question from Mohd Nazri Abu Hassan (PN-Merbok) on Malaysia’s preparedness to face an ageing society by 2040, including elderly welfare, healthcare, social protection and family support, Akmal Nasrullah said the country attained ageing nation status in 2021.

items.title: Anwar wants AIM to provide microcredit financing for youth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM) has been asked to expand access to microcredit financing for young people. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the microcredit agency would be encouraged to explore ways to improve accessibility for young borrowers through more structured and efficient management and repayment methods. Syed Saddiq said the proposed scheme could provide financing between RM10,000 and RM30,000 with a one-off management fee and flexible repayment arrangements similar to AIM.

items.title: MoE weighs centralising Malaysia’s preschool management as it plans 1,040 new classes by 2027
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — In a major expansion of early childhood education, the Ministry of Education (MOE) plans to roll out 1,040 new preschool classes by 2027, marking a key milestone for the Madani government’s five-year tenure. According to the MOE, the initiative, launched in 2023, reflects the government’s commitment to expanding early childhood education and ensuring no child misses out on quality learning, regardless of their location or background. As of May 31, the ministry is operating 10,491 preschool classes nationwide, benefiting 217,026 students.

items.title: Data centre jobs can pay up to RM30,000 a month, says Gobind
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's data centre boom is creating high-skilled jobs offering salaries of up to RM30,000 a month, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. "Based on the Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) report, From Bytes to Bucks: The Economics of Data Centres in Malaysia, published in the third quarter of 2025, data centre development contributes to the creation of high-skilled jobs such as network engineers, cloud architects and data scientists, with salaries above the national average," he said. He said the Data Centre Task Force (DCTF) was established as a national-level coordination and assessment mechanism for all proposed data centre developments.

items.title: Malaysia’s holiday homes generated RM2.2b in 2025, first official DOSM data shows
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — A total of 71,101 holiday homes were operating nationwide in 2025, generating an estimated RM2.2 billion in revenue, according to the Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 published by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today. “The holiday homes industry continues to create positive economic spillover effects through higher demand for accommodation, transportation, food and beverage services, retail trade and recreational activities. The Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 is the first official statistical publication on holiday homes by state and district in Malaysia.

items.title: Democrats push key US Senate candidate to quit over sex assault claim
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Top Democrats called on scandal-hit US Senate candidate Graham Platner to quit the race on Monday after a sexual assault allegation threatened to derail one of the party's best chances of flipping a Republican-held seat. Platner, a Marine veteran, oysterman and political newcomer who won the state's Democratic primary last month, rejected the allegation. The Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) said it would pull funding for the Maine Senate race if Platner remains on the ballot.

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar says Sunday voting remarks were personal view, not pressure on Election Commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stressed that his remarks regarding the Johor state election dates were merely a personal view aimed at encouraging a higher voter turnout, and not an attempt to interfere in the Election Commission’s (EC) decision. He said the EC was an independent body with the sole authority to determine polling dates, although he believed Sunday voting would make it easier for Malaysians working in Singapore to return home to cast their ballots. “Many Malaysians are working in Singapore, and many of them work half-day on Saturdays.

items.title: Johor polls: Early voting turnout passes 56pc by 11am
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — More than half of the early voters in Johor’s 16th state election, comprising security personnel, had cast their ballots by 11am today. A total of 64 polling centres opened at 8am for the registered voters to cast their votes. A total of 172 candidates are vying for 56 state assembly seats in the Johor polls, with some 2.7 million voters set to cast their ballots this Saturday.

items.title: At least two more years before Malaysia gets new LCS missiles, says army chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia will need at least two more years to acquire a replacement surface-to-surface missile system for the navy’s upcoming littoral combat ships (LCS), Chief of Defence Force Gen Datuk Malek Razak Sulaiman said today. However, Malek Razak said the delay would not hinder the navy’s maritime readiness, as the LCS could first be equipped with existing weapons systems. He said Malaysia would also showcase the LCS vessels alongside other recently procured defence assets, including the ANKA-S unmanned aerial vehicles and FA-50 fighter jets, at the Langkawi International Maritime and Aerospace Exhibition 2027 (LIMA’27) in April next year.

items.title: Anwar says govt will look into petrol retailers’ claims of losses from price fluctuations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today promised to heed petrol station operators’ concerns over alleged losses stemming from oil price fluctuations. Anwar told Parliament this morning that he would instruct Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan to meet them and obtain more information. “I will ask finance minister II to help obtain more details, as well as discuss this with oil companies,” Anwar said in reply to a supplementary question by Ipoh Timur MP Howard Lee Chuan How.

items.title: Azalina dismisses claims that BN victory in Johor would free Najib from prison
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — Elections cannot be used as a channel to free any individual who is serving a prison sentence, said Umno information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. Azalina, who is also Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), said there is no law stating that elections can be used to release any particular individual. Instead, she said the power to grant pardons lies solely with the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.

items.title: Sabah hospital achieves milestone with complex surgery separating 28-day-old conjoined twins
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 7 — The Sabah Women and Children’s Hospital (HWKKS) in Likas has achieved a historic first after successfully performing a separation surgery on pygopagus conjoined twins, marking a major advancement for specialised paediatric care in the state. Pygopagus twins are conjoined at the lower back, specifically the sacrum, and may share parts of the neural tube, lower digestive tract and genital organs. The surgery involved specialists from multiple disciplines, including neonatology, anaesthesiology, paediatric surgery, neurosurgery, spinal surgery, plastic and reconstructive surgery, and radiology, with additional support from specialist teams from Peninsular Malaysia.

items.title: TNB powering 36 data centres as of Q1 2026, says Energy Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has supplied electricity to 36 operating data centres with a planned supply capacity of around 4.5 gigawatts (GW) through the first quarter (1Q) of 2026, according to the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (Petra). The ministry said TNB has also met power demand for 23 data centre projects currently under construction, with a maximum demand capacity of 3.8 GW. In the context of the country’s green energy transition, PETRA said the government remains committed to ensuring that the country’s electricity supply capacity mix is optimised through the use of 70 per cent clean energy sources, which includes 58 per cent renewable energy (RE), 10 per cent hydrogen, and two per cent nuclear by 2050 as decided by JPPPET Meeting No 1/2026.

items.title: Adaptive reuse project to breathe new life into George Town heritage shophouses
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 7 — The adaptive reuse of eight Category II heritage shophouses into the Cultural Heritage Hub (CH2) is set to become a model for preserving George Town’s ageing pre-war shophouses. George Town World Heritage Incorporated (GTWHI) general manager Ang Ming Chee said the project would show how adaptive reuse can breathe new life into old buildings while respecting their heritage value. "There are about 371 Category II shophouses in George Town,” she said in her speech during the kick-off ceremony for CH2 today.

items.title: Teo Nie Ching urges Onn Hafiz to explain alleged campaigning at Johor TVET event
items.summary: KULAI, July 7 — Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has been urged to explain allegations that a group of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) students was required to attend a programme that was later used to canvass support for Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor state election. Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching claimed the party had received complaints from parents and students alleging they were instructed to attend the Johor Mara TVET Roadshow at the Inland Revenue Board (LHDN) Hall in Kluang on July 4. Teo, who is also Deputy Communications Minister, claimed they were warned that failure to attend would be treated as absenteeism, but alleged the programme later turned into a platform to garner support for the BN candidate.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s press freedom ranking fell due to enforcement, not censorship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today attributed Malaysia’s drop in the 2026 World Press Freedom Index to action taken against certain media companies, while denying claims that it was due to censorship. Malaysia was ranked 95th this year, down from 88th in 2025, in the index compiled by Reporters Without Borders (RSF). Anwar insisted today that press freedom in Malaysia remains “relatively better” than in comparable countries.

items.title: George Town’s Unesco status not under threat, says Penang exco
items.summary: Wong said George Town’s World Heritage Site remains in a healthy state as the city marks the 18th anniversary of its inscription as a Unesco World Heritage Site. While existing guidelines and the Penang State Heritage Enactment continue to safeguard heritage buildings, Wong said the state is now broadening its focus to include intangible cultural heritage. As part of that effort, Penang gazetted 50 items as state heritage this year, comprising 28 heritage foods, 15 heritage sites and seven elements of intangible cultural heritage.

items.title: GOF busts migrant smuggling operation in Kelantan, arrests 10 foreigners
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 7 — The Eighth Battalion of the General Operations Force (GOF) has crippled a migrant smuggling operation with the arrest of 10 foreign nationals, including a Thai national believed to have acted as the transporter, at Jalan Kampung Kubang Bemban, Pengkalan Kubor, Tumpat. Upon apprehending the suspect, he said the patrol team found nine foreign nationals, comprising seven Chinese nationals and two Myanmar nationals aged between 22 and 31, who failed to produce valid travel documents inside the car. Ahmad Radzi said the total value of the seized items, including the car, was estimated at RM77,100 and the case is being investigated under Section 26J of the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007, Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Section 15(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.

items.title: Anwar: Govt approved RM6b in loans, guarantees to help SMEs cope with war fallout
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government has approved up to RM6 billion in loans and guarantees to small and medium enterprises (SMEs) under two programmes to contain the impact of the war in the Middle East, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim told Parliament today. Under the SME Stabilisation Relief Facility, RM1 billion in loans has been approved by Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) since its roll-out last month, benefiting some 1,500 companies, Anwar said. “Microcredit loans through agencies such as BSN, Agrobank and Tekun also reached RM2.2 billion, benefiting 195,000 micro entrepreneurs,” Anwar said.

items.title: Heavy rain fails to dampen Johor early voting at police HQ
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — A heavy downpour at about 9.40am today did little to disrupt the smooth conduct of early voting for the 16th Johor state election at the State Police Contingent Headquarters (IPK) here today. Police personnel arriving to cast their ballots were seen hurrying into the polling centre to avoid the rain, but voting continued as normal. Despite the downpour, a steady stream of officers and personnel continued arriving at the polling centre to cast their ballots.

items.title: Johor polls early voting turnout reaches 38.64pc as of 10am
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Early voting turnout for the Johor state election reached 38.64 per cent by 10am today. A total of 64 polling centres opened at 8am for 20,607 registered voters to cast their votes. For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor.

items.title: No penalties for voluntary e-Invoice corrections until end-2027, says PM Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The government plans to introduce an e-Invoicing Voluntary Disclosure Programme until December 31, 2027, allowing taxpayers to correct submitted or omitted e-Invoice transactions without penalties from the Inland Revenue Board. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the programme would give taxpayers the opportunity to review, update and make comprehensive corrections to e-Invoice transactions that had been or should have been submitted from the mandatory implementation date until the programme ends. Anwar said the government also agreed to accelerate tax incentives for taxpayers who fully comply with e-Invoicing by allowing full capital allowance claims within one year on qualifying expenditure related to its implementation.
